title: boykinia occidentalis var. elata
intro: Boykinia occidentalis var. elata, also known as Western bog ginger or tall bog ginger, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Boykinia occidentalis var. elata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as coughs and colds, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
